title_fr: Algérie — polygones des limites de wilaya (69)

properties.code: wilaya code as an integer 1–69 (join key to wilayas.json)

sources.name: OpenStreetMap — administrative boundaries (relation, boundary=administrative, admin_level=4) in Algeria

provenance_notes: Imported verbatim from the GeoAlgeria app repo, commit 300cb85 (main), blob 92642be34c678e243e664a55ca7a373dd1f08e94 — byte-identical, geometry untouched.

The OSM extraction itself landed in the app repo on 2026-06-21 in commit f107d6a ('fix: replace GADM wilaya boundaries with OSM data (69 wilayas)'), which records the source (OSM admin_level=4 relations), the licence (ODbL) and the simplification (mapshaper dp 2%, keep-shapes). The bytes have not changed since.

retrieved = 2026-06-21 is the commit date of that extraction and therefore an UPPER BOUND on the Overpass pull date. The file carries no timestamp_osm_base or any other provenance field (properties are `code` only), and no fetch script for it exists in either repo, so the exact pull date cannot be established. It is not a guess at a narrower date.

The commonly quoted '~150 m' figure describes the COORDINATE QUANTISATION (3 decimal places ≈ 111 m in latitude, ≈ 96 m in longitude at 30°N), not the simplification error. Douglas-Peucker at 2% left a median vertex spacing of 3.4 km, so the polygon can depart from the true border by far more than 150 m along any simplified stretch. Treat these as display-grade boundaries (good to about z11), not as a survey-grade border test.

Superseded predecessor: the same file was built from GADM 4.1 before f107d6a; app-repo scripts/prepare-boundaries.mjs still builds that version and must never be re-run. An unverified OSM re-extraction is parked on the app-repo branch chore/osm-boundaries and was deliberately NOT used here.
Data licence is ODbL-1.0 even though the geoalgeria npm package is MIT — the same split the repo already uses for its other OSM-derived packages (ecoles, mosquees, pharmacies, tourisme).
